{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

:::{#exr-ci-OR}
Given
a maximum likelihood estimate $\hb$
and
a corresponding estimated covariance matrix 
$\hS \eqdef \widehat{\Covt}(\hb)$, 
calculate a 95% confidence interval for the odds ratio 
comparing covariate patterns $\vx$ and $\vxs$, $\ror(\vx,\vxs)$.
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#sol-ci-OR}

::: notes
By [the central limit theorem for MLEs](intro-MLEs.qmd#thm-dist-mle),
a 95% confidence interval for $\ror(\vx,\vxs)$
can be constructed as:

$$\hat\ror \pm 1.96 * \HSE{\hat\ror}$${#eq-ci-OR-direct}
However, $\HSE{\hat\ror}$ seems difficult to compute;
doing so would require using the 
[delta method](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Delta_method).

Instead, using the invariance property of MLEs,
we can first calculate a confidence interval for the logarithm of the odds ratio,

$$\logf{\ror(\vx,\vxs)} \in (L,R)$${#eq-or-diff-logodds}

and then exponentiate the endpoints of that log-odds-scale confidence interval:
:::

$$\ror(\vx,\vxs) \in (e^L, e^R)$${#eq-ci-OR-exp}

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exr-or-diff-logodds}

Find a 95% confidence interval for the natural logarithm of the odds ratio,
$\logf{\ror(\vx,\vxs)}$

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#sol-or-diff-logodds}

From @cor-log-or, we know:

$$\logf{\ror(\vx,\vxs)} = \difflogodds$$

By [the central limit theorem for MLEs](intro-MLEs.qmd#thm-dist-mle),
a 95% confidence interval for $\difflogodds$
can be constructed as:

$$\hdifflogodds \pm 1.96 * \teal{\HSE{\hdifflogodds}}$$

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exr-se-diff-logodds}

::: notes
How can we estimate the standard error of
$\hdifflogodds$?
:::

$$\teal{\HSE{\hdifflogodds}} = \teal{?}$$

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#sol-se-diff-logodds}

$$
\green{\SE{\hdifflogodds}} = 
\sqrt{\red{\Var{\hdifflogodds}}}
$${#eq-se-sqrt-var}

By @lem-diff-logodds and [the variance of a linear combination](probability.qmd#thm-var-lincom):

$$
\ba
\red{\Var{\hdifflogodds}}
&= \Var{(\diffvx) \cdot \hb}
\\
&= \pt{\diffvx}\Cov{\hb}(\diffvx)
\\
&= \red{\pt{\diffvx}\Sigma(\diffvx)}
\ea
$${#eq-var-diff-logodds}

where $\Sigma \eqdef \Covt(\hb)$.

{{< slidebreak >}}

::: notes
Expanding @eq-var-diff-logodds out of matrix-vector notation, we have:
:::

$$
\ba
\red{\pt{\diffvx}\Sigma(\diffvx)}
&= \sumip\sumjp (\diffvx)_i \Sigma_{ij} (\diffvx)_j
\\
&= \sumip\sumjp (\Delta x_i) \Sigma_{ij} (\Delta x_j)
\\
&= \red{\sumip\sumjp (x_i - x^*_i) \Covt(\hb_i,\hb_j) (x_j - x^*_j)}
\ea
$$

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

Combining @eq-var-diff-logodds with the invariance property of MLEs
gives an estimator for the variance and standard error:

:::{#thm-est-se-diff-logodds}

#### Estimated variance and standard error of difference in log-odds

$$
\orange{\hVar{\Delta{\hat\logodds}}} = \orange{\tp{\diffvx}\hS(\diffvx)}
$${#eq-est-var-difflogodds}

$$
\teal{\HSE{\Delta{\hat\logodds}}} =
\teal{\sqrt{\tp{\diffvx}\hS(\diffvx)}}
$${#eq-est-se-diff-logodds}

:::

::: proof
By @sol-se-diff-logodds,
$\Var{\hdifflogodds} = \tp{\diffvx}\Sigma(\diffvx)$ (@eq-var-diff-logodds).
By the invariance property of MLEs,
we estimate $\Sigma$ with $\hS \eqdef \widehat{\Covt}(\hb)$,
which yields @eq-est-var-difflogodds;
taking the square root gives @eq-est-se-diff-logodds.
:::

::: notes
Compare this result with [confidence intervals](Linear-models-overview.qmd#sec-se-fitted).
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exm-est-se-diff-logodds-beetles}

#### Standard error and CI for a beetle odds ratio

::: notes
We instantiate @thm-est-se-diff-logodds
for the `beetles` logistic regression model
(@tbl-beetles-model-ungrouped).
:::

Compare two covariate patterns,
a dose of 1.8 versus a dose of 1.7:
$\vx = (1, 1.8)\'$
and
$\vxs = (1, 1.7)\'$,
so that $\diffvx = \vx - \vxs = (0, 0.1)\'$.

```{r}
#| label: exm-se-diff-logodds-beetles-setup
#| code-fold: show
delta_x <- c(0, 0.1)
sigma_hat <- vcov(beetles_glm_ungrouped)
diff_logodds_hat <- as.numeric(delta_x %*% coef(beetles_glm_ungrouped))
se_diff <- sqrt(as.numeric(t(delta_x) %*% sigma_hat %*% delta_x))
ci_diff <- diff_logodds_hat + c(-1, 1) * 1.96 * se_diff
```

By @eq-est-se-diff-logodds,
the estimated standard error of the difference in log-odds is
$\HSE{\Delta\hat\logodds} = \sqrt{\tp{\diffvx}\hS(\diffvx)} = `r se_diff`$,
and the estimated difference in log-odds is
$\Delta\hat\logodds = (\diffvx)\cdot\hb = `r diff_logodds_hat`$.

The resulting 95% confidence interval for $\difflogodds$ is
$(`r ci_diff[1]`, `r ci_diff[2]`)$,
so by @eq-ci-OR-exp
the estimated odds ratio and its 95% confidence interval are
$\hat\ror(\vx,\vxs) = e^{`r diff_logodds_hat`} = `r exp(diff_logodds_hat)`$,
with CI $(`r exp(ci_diff[1])`, `r exp(ci_diff[2])`)$.

:::
